Name: AController
Description:
Controllers are non-physical actors that can possess a Pawn to control
  its actions.  PlayerControllers are used by human players to control pawns, while
  AIControllers implement the artificial intelligence for the pawns they control.
  Controllers take control of a pawn using their Possess() method, and relinquish
  control of the pawn by calling UnPossess().
 
  Controllers receive notifications for many of the events occurring for the Pawn they
  are controlling.  This gives the controller the opportunity to implement the behavior
  in response to this event, intercepting the event and superseding the Pawn's default
  behavior.
 
  ControlRotation (accessed via GetControlRotation()), determines the viewingaiming
  direction of the controlled Pawn and is affected by input such as from a mouse or gamepad.

Variables.Name: Pawn
Variables.Description: Pawn currently being controlled by this controller.  Use Pawn.Possess() to take control of a pawn

Variables.Name: Character
Variables.Description: Character currently being controlled by this controller.  Value is same as Pawn if the controlled pawn is a character, otherwise NULL

Variables.Name: PlayerState
Variables.Description: PlayerState containing replicated information about the player using this controller (only exists for players, not NPCs).

Variables.Name: TransformComponent
Variables.Description: Component to give controllers a transform and enable attachment if desired.

Variables.Name: ControlRotation
Variables.Description: The control rotation of the Controller. See GetControlRotation.

Variables.Name: bAttachToPawn
Variables.Description:
If true, the controller location will match the possessed Pawn's location. If false, it will not be updated. Rotation will match ControlRotation in either case.
	  Since a Controller's location is normally inaccessible, this is intended mainly for purposes of being able to attach
	  an Actor that follows the possessed Pawn location, but that still has the full aim rotation (since a Pawn might
	  update only some components of the rotation).

Variables.Name: bIsPlayerController
Variables.Description: Whether this controller is a PlayerController.

Variables.Name: IgnoreMoveInput
Variables.Description: Ignores movement input. Stacked state storage, Use accessor function IgnoreMoveInput()

Variables.Name: IgnoreLookInput
Variables.Description: Ignores look input. Stacked state storage, use accessor function IgnoreLookInput().

Functions.Name: GetControlRotation
Functions.Description:
Get the control rotation. This is the full aim rotation, which may be different than a camera orientation (for example in a third person view),
	   and may differ from the rotation of the controlled Pawn (which may choose not to visually pitch or roll, for example).

Functions.Name: SetControlRotation
Functions.Description: Set the control rotation. The RootComponent's rotation will also be updated to match it if RootComponent->bAbsoluteRotation is true.

Functions.Name: SetInitialLocationAndRotation
Functions.Description: Set the initial location and rotation of the controller, as well as the control rotation. Typically used when the controller is first created.

Functions.Name: SetStartSpot
Functions.Description: Set the StartSpot

Functions.Name: ClearStartSpot
Functions.Description: Clear the StartSpot

Functions.Name: LineOfSightTo
Functions.Description: Checks line to center and top of other actor

Params.Name: Other
Params.Description: is the actor whose visibility is being checked.

Params.Name: ViewPoint
Params.Description: is eye position visibility is being checked from. If vect(0,0,0) passed in, uses current viewtarget's eye position.

Return.Description: true if controller's pawn can see Other actor.

Functions.Name: OnRep_Pawn
Functions.Description: Replication Notification Callbacks

Functions.Name: CastToPlayerController
Functions.Description: DEPRECATED! Use the standard "Cast To" node instead. Casts this Controller to a Player Controller, if possible.

Functions.Name: ClientSetLocation
Functions.Description: Replicated function to set the pawn location and rotation, allowing server to force (ex. teleports).

Functions.Name: ClientSetRotation
Functions.Description: Replicated function to set the pawn rotation, allowing the server to force.

Functions.Name: K2_GetPawn
Functions.Description: Return the Pawn that is currently 'controlled' by this PlayerController

Functions.Name: GetViewTarget
Functions.Description: Get the actor the controller is looking at

Functions.Name: GetDesiredRotation
Functions.Description: Get the desired pawn target rotation

Functions.Name: IsPlayerController
Functions.Description: Returns whether this Controller is a PlayerController.

Functions.Name: IsLocalPlayerController
Functions.Description: Returns whether this Controller is a locally controlled PlayerController.

Functions.Name: IsLocalController
Functions.Description: Returns whether this Controller is a local controller.

Functions.Name: Possess
Functions.Description:
Handles attaching this controller to the specified pawn.
	  Only runs on the network authority (where HasAuthority() returns true).

Functions.Name: UnPossess
Functions.Description: Called to unpossess our pawn for any reason that is not the pawn being destroyed (destruction handled by PawnDestroyed()).

Functions.Name: StopMovement
Functions.Description: Aborts the move the controller is currently performing

Functions.Name: SetIgnoreMoveInput
Functions.Description: Locks or unlocks movement input, consecutive calls stack up and require the same amount of calls to undo, or can all be undone using ResetIgnoreMoveInput.

Params.Name: bNewMoveInput
Params.Description: If true, move input is ignored. If false, input is not ignored.

Functions.Name: ResetIgnoreMoveInput
Functions.Description: Stops ignoring move input by resetting the ignore move input state.

Functions.Name: IsMoveInputIgnored
Functions.Description: Returns true if movement input is ignored.

Functions.Name: SetIgnoreLookInput
Functions.Description: Locks or unlocks look input, consecutive calls stack up and require the same amount of calls to undo, or can all be undone using ResetIgnoreLookInput.

Params.Name: bNewLookInput
Params.Description: If true, look input is ignored. If false, input is not ignored.

Functions.Name: ResetIgnoreLookInput
Functions.Description: Stops ignoring look input by resetting the ignore look input state.

Functions.Name: IsLookInputIgnored
Functions.Description: Returns true if look input is ignored.

Functions.Name: ResetIgnoreInputFlags
Functions.Description: Reset move and look input ignore flags.

Functions.Name: ReceiveInstigatedAnyDamage
Functions.Description: Event when this controller instigates ANY damage

Delegate.Name: OnInstigatedAnyDamage
Delegate.Description: Called when the controller has instigated damage in any way
